name: Beef Chow Fun with Black Bean Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 green bell pepper, seeded and cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------BEEF MARINADE-----------------------
2 tablespoons dark soy sauce
2 tablespoons rice wine or dry sherry
1/2 teaspoon sugar
1 teaspoon cornstarch
1 teaspoon peanut or sesame oil
1. Cut the flank steak into 1/4-inch thick, 1 1/2-inch long, and 1/2-inch wide slices.
2. Place the beef slices in a bowl and toss with the beef marinade mixture. Set aside.
3. Cover the black beans with water until soft (about 5 minutes). Drain and rinse off excess salt.
4. Mash the black beans together with garlic and ginger into a coarse paste; set aside.
5. Separate the strands of rice noodles. Set aside.
6. Heat a wok over high heat until hot, then add half the oil and swirl to coat the sides.
7. Add the beef; toss until the beef loses its redness (about 30-45 seconds). Remove to a bowl.
8. Add the remaining oil to the hot wok.
9. Stir-fry the black bean mixture until fragrant (about 10 seconds).
10. Over high heat, add the onions and peppers; stir-fry until they begin to soften but remain crisp (about 1 minute).
11. Add the rice noodles; stir-fry to coat the strands with seasoned oil (about 30 seconds).
12. Add white pepper, sugar, and soy sauce; toss until evenly distributed.
13. Return the beef; toss a few turns to mix in.
14. Transfer to a serving platter and serve immediately.
